questions.text: Which of the following is most likely to be produced using flow production?
Which of the following describes the process of managing the providers of resources at the different stages in the production process?
Which of the following is a cost of maintaining quality?
Which of the following describes the process of choosing suppliers and negotiating the overall supplier/business contract alongside the payment terms?
Identify two ways a business can identify quality problems.
Explain one advantage of job production.
State and explain two methods of post sales service.
Using Item A, calculate the average number of tea bags a tea drinker in the UK uses each day in one year. Show your workings.
Explain one customer expectation of quality when buying products from Yorkshire Tea.
Analyse one benefit to Yorkshire Tea of effective logistics throughout the tea production process.
Recommend whether Taylors should increase the amount of customer engagement activities for its Yorkshire Tea brand. Give reasons for your recommendation.

mark_points.criteria: Award 1 mark for the correct option.
Award 1 mark for the correct option.
Award 1 mark for the correct option.
Award 1 mark for the correct option.

mark_schemes.guidance: Answers may include: Mystery shoppers; Customer service surveys; Number of repeat sales; Amount of returned products; Volume of complaints from customers; Quality control checks; Quality assurance; Total quality management (TQM)

mark_points.criteria: Acceptable: Mystery shoppers / Customer service surveys / Number of repeat sales / Amount of returned products / Volume of complaints from customers / Quality control checks / Quality assurance / Total quality management (TQM)
mark_points.description: Identifies a valid way a business can identify quality problems

mark_points.criteria: Acceptable: Mystery shoppers / Customer service surveys / Number of repeat sales / Amount of returned products / Volume of complaints from customers / Quality control checks / Quality assurance / Total quality management (TQM)
mark_points.description: Identifies a second valid way a business can identify quality problems

mark_schemes.guidance: Possible answers may include: Able to meet the exact requirements of customers; Highly flexible; High levels of customer satisfaction; Increased levels of employee motivation; Products are usually high quality; Unique products can be produced; High prices can be charged; Businesses are able to differentiate their products from rivals.

mark_points.criteria: Acceptable: Able to meet the exact requirements of customers / Highly flexible / High levels of customer satisfaction / Increased levels of employee motivation / Products are usually high quality / Unique products can be produced / High prices can be charged / Businesses are able to differentiate their products from rivals
mark_points.description: Identifies an advantage of job production

mark_points.criteria: Award 1 mark for a linked explanation or consequence that develops the identified point.

mark_schemes.guidance: Answers may include: User training; Help lines; Servicing; Warranties/guarantees; Exchanging/refunding goods that do not meet customer needs or are faulty; Efficient delivery of products.

mark_points.criteria: Acceptable: User training / Help lines / Servicing / Warranties/guarantees / Exchanging/refunding goods that do not meet customer needs or are faulty / Efficient delivery of products
mark_points.description: States a method of post sales service

mark_points.criteria: Award 1 mark for some development of the stated method.
Acceptable: User training / Help lines / Servicing / Warranties/guarantees / Exchanging/refunding goods that do not meet customer needs or are faulty / Efficient delivery of products
mark_points.description: States a second method of post sales service

mark_points.criteria: Award 1 mark for some development of the stated method.

mark_schemes.guidance: 2 marks for calculation. 4 tea bags (2). Method 1: 1,460/365 (1) = 4 tea bags (1). Method 2: 1 460/52/7 (1) = 4.01 tea bags (1).

mark_points.criteria: Award 1 mark for showing a correct method (e.g., 1,460/365 or 1,460/52/7).
Award 1 mark for the correct answer (4 tea bags or 4.01 tea bags).

mark_schemes.guidance: Answers may include: Flavour; Taste; Value for money; Sustainability; Environmental issues; Ethical trading.

levels.descriptor: Sound understanding and application of the topics. Applies knowledge and understanding to the context sufficiently.
Basic understanding and application of the topics. Applies basic knowledge and understanding to the context.

mark_schemes.guidance: Answers may include: Freshness and quality of tea can be assured; Tea tasters and production line workers will not be idle; Yorkshire Tea sells its products both in the UK and internationally.

levels.descriptor: Detailed analysis of topics based on the context. Business areas are fully analysed.
Sound analysis of topics based on the context. Business areas are partially analysed.
Basic analysis of topics based on the context. Basic analysis of business areas.

mark_schemes.guidance: Answers may include: The relationship that exists between a business and its customers; Ways in which a business interacts with its customers; Emotional connection between a customer and a business.

mark_points.criteria: Acceptable: The relationship that exists between a business and its customers / Ways in which a business interacts with its customers / Emotional connection between a customer and a business / Positive customer engagement can lead to a high degree of brand loyalty/higher sales / Social media is often used as a way to connect and engage with customers
mark_points.description: Gives a limited description of customer engagement

mark_points.criteria: Award 1 mark for some development of the description.
mark_points.description: Develops the description of customer engagement

mark_schemes.guidance: Recommend whether Taylors should increase the amount of customer engagement activities for its Yorkshire Tea brand.

levels.descriptor: Detailed analysis and evaluation of topics based on the context. Sustained line of reasoning, coherent, relevant, substantiated with a focused conclusion.
Sound analysis and evaluation of topics based on the context. A line of reasoning with a conclusion that has some justification.
Basic analysis and evaluation of topics based on the context. Basic line of reasoning with a conclusion.
